applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused elementwise kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Fused AvgPool1d + GELU + Scale + Max into a single Triton kernel. Uses exp-based tanh implementation since tl.math.tanh is unavailable on ROCm. Matmul remains using torch.nn.Linear (rocBLAS) as it dominates 96% of runtime and naive Triton matmul is much slower.

attempted_but_failed.name: Fully fused Triton matmul
attempted_but_failed.reason: Naive scalar-loop Triton matmul implementation was 163x slower (155ms vs 0.95ms) than rocBLAS
attempted_but_failed.error: Scalar loops in Triton cannot compete with vendor-optimized BLAS libraries

attempted_but_failed.name: Scalar Triton elementwise kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Processing one element at a time instead of vectorized blocks was 1.8x slower (1.74ms vs 0.947ms)
attempted_but_failed.error: Lack of memory coalescing and vectorization

notes: Matmul (GEMM) dominates at 96.43% of CUDA time - this is the fundamental bottleneck
rocBLAS/hipBLAS via torch.nn.Linear is highly optimized and cannot be beaten by naive Triton
Fusing the remaining 3.57% (pool+gelu+scale+max) gives minimal theoretical speedup
tl.math.tanh is unavailable on ROCm - must use exp-based tanh formula
BLOCK_SIZE should be power of 2 and align with wavefront size (64 for MI355X)
Current implementation achieves SCORE: 60.0 with ~1.002x speedup

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent produced no output for the 3rd consecutive trial. Providing complete ready-to-write code that algebraically fuses AvgPool into the GEMM weights, reducing the matmul from (1024,8192)@(8192,8192)

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
